spec_match.rationale: Builds a VideoFrame for each of the 8 rotation × flip combinations, reads vf.rotation and vf.flip on each, and renders the rotated canvas alongside — accurate to the spec's IDL for VideoFrameInit { rotation, flip }.

interactive.rationale: Generate / Clear buttons drive the gallery; each card runs a live VideoFrame construction and reads back metadata. Past the static-card bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises all eight orientations side-by-side with live `VideoFrame.rotation` readout; the page delivers that.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers all 8 rotation × flip combinations and degrades gracefully when VideoFrame.rotation is absent. Misses: codedWidth/displayWidth distinction in the per-card meta, the order-of-operations (the page applies flip BEFORE rotate which matches spec — good — but never says so), and any failure-mode for invalid rotation values like 45 or -90 (spec only allows 0/90/180/270).

references.rationale: No see-also section. The chromestatus.com/feature/5128855343595520 reference is not on the page at all. Invariant #3 violation.

design_system.rationale: Two raw hex values applied as CSS: `sb.style.background = hasRotation ? '#d4f5e2' : '#fde8e8'` in the support-badge probe, and the canvas `fillStyle = '#f0f0f0'` for the card backgrounds (inside bitmap — acceptable). The first is a real CSS variable bypass that breaks invariant #5; should be `var(--accent-emerald)` / `var(--accent-rose)` with `var(--bg-ivory)` text.

openQuestions.title: Add the missing chromestatus + spec references
openQuestions.detail: The page has no see-also or references block. Per invariant #3, every concept page must include https://chromestatus.com/feature/5128855343595520. Add a see-also section with that link, the WebCodecs spec, and links to the other three sibling concepts.

openQuestions.title: Replace the raw `#d4f5e2` / `#fde8e8` background colours
openQuestions.detail: The support-badge JS sets `style.background` to raw hex values for the pass/fail states. This bypasses the design-system variables (invariant #5). Replace with `var(--accent-emerald)` (yes) and `var(--accent-rose)` (no), pair with `var(--bg-ivory)` text. Also add a className flip rather than inline style so CSS owns the design.

openQuestions.title: Add codedWidth vs displayWidth per card
openQuestions.detail: Per spec, at rotation 90/270 the VideoFrame's displayWidth and displayHeight swap relative to codedWidth/codedHeight. The gallery currently only prints `display: WxH`. Add a second meta line printing `coded: WxH` from the VideoFrame instance so the swap is visible.

openQuestions.title: Probe rejection of invalid rotation values
openQuestions.detail: The spec only allows 0, 90, 180, 270. A small subsection that attempts new VideoFrame(..., {rotation: 45}) and shows the resulting throw/coerce behaviour would teach the constraint and is a useful failure-mode demo.

openQuestions.title: Label as EXIF orientations 1–8
openQuestions.detail: The eight combinations correspond exactly to the eight EXIF orientation values (1=normal, 2=mirror-h, 3=180, 4=mirror-v, 5=mirror-h+90CCW, 6=90CW, 7=mirror-h+90CW, 8=90CCW). Annotate each card with its EXIF number so the page becomes a reference for camera metadata, not just a transform demo.

summary: Wide coverage — all 8 orientations probed live with a real VideoFrame construct on each — but two material problems: no chromestatus/spec links anywhere on the page (invariant #3), and the support-badge uses raw hex backgrounds instead of design-system variables (invariant #5). Both are easy fixes.
